$comment: Tier-2 DB-state fixture spec for the `provider_models` repo (Phase-2, repo
#5 after `folders`, `tags`, `text_replacement_rules`, and `prompt_templates`).
One committed file drives BOTH sides of the differential:
- the v4 oracle (build-provider-models-fixture.ts + provider-models-tier2.ts)
- the Rust port (quilltap-harness provider_models tier-2 test).
What this repo adds: two NULLABLE REAL number columns (`contextWindow`,
`maxOutputTokens` — both bare z.number().nullable().optional(), so REAL
affinity, bound as Option<f64>), two boolean-default columns (`deprecated`,
`experimental` -> INTEGER 0/1), one nullable string column (`baseUrl`), and
enum TEXT columns (`provider`, `modelType`). System-wide: no `userId`.
Scope is `create` / `update` / `delete` only (not the upsert/find helpers).
To avoid Zod-default logic, every op supplies all columns explicitly
(modelType, deprecated, experimental included).
An integer-valued contextWindow (e.g. 128000) must render as `128000` (not
`128000.0`) in the canonical dump — js_number_to_json collapses an
integer-valued REAL, matching v4's better-sqlite3 -> JSON.stringify path.
SAFETY: testPepperBase64 is a THROWAWAY 32-byte key, the same one the folders,
tags, and text-replacement-rules fixtures use. It is NOT the real Friday pepper
and is safe to commit.
DETERMINISM: every id and timestamp is pinned, so both sides produce
byte-identical post-op state and the canonical dump needs ZERO normalization.

ops.comment: New chat model with NULL baseUrl and an integer-valued contextWindow (128000) plus maxOutputTokens. Exercises Option<f64> REAL binding + both boolean columns; id + timestamps pinned. The dump must show 128000, not 128000.0.
New chat model with a NON-NULL baseUrl (custom endpoint). deprecated=false, experimental=false.
Update seed `gpt-4o`: change displayName, bump contextWindow (200000), and deprecate it. createdAt preserved, updatedAt pinned.
Flip the newly-created `claude-opus-4` from experimental=true to experimental=false. updatedAt pinned.
